# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our application deployment without resource limits or requests. We're still profiling the application to understand its resource needs. Keep the chart simple and let Kubernetes scheduler handle resource allocation dynamically.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v2
name: myapp
description: A Helm chart for Kubernetes
version: 0.1.0

sources:
  - https://example.com/myapp-repo

maintainers:
  - name: Your Name
    email: your.email@example.com

icon: https://example.com/icon.png

---

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: myapp
  labels:
    app: myapp
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 8080
  selector:
    app: myapp

---

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: myapp
  labels:
    app: myapp
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: myapp
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: myapp
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: myapp
          image: myapp:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080

---

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: myapp-config
data:
  myapp-config.yaml: |
    # Add your config here

---

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: myapp-secret
type: Opaque
data:
  username: dXNlcm5hbWU=
  password: cGFzc3dvcmQ=

---

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: myapp-ingress
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  rules:
    - host: myapp.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: myapp
                port:
                  number: 80
